subject: Geography
title: The US-Mexico Border Wall
description: Learn The US-Mexico Border Wall in one minute.

hook.text: The US-Mexico border stretches 3,145 km across deserts, rivers, and cities.

buildup.text: Fencing covers roughly a third of the border. The rest is open desert or river.

discovery.text: Tunnels, ladders, and catapults have all been used to bypass existing barriers.

twist.text: Most undocumented immigrants actually arrive legally and overstay visas, not cross the border.

climax.text: The wall debate is as much about symbolism and politics as it is about geography.

punchline.text: Building a wall is easy. Solving migration is not.

quiz.question: How do most undocumented immigrants enter the US?
options: They arrive legally and overstay visas
They cross the southern border on foot
